title: petrophile ericifolia subsp. subpubescens
intro: Petrophile ericifolia subsp. subpubescens, also known as the hairy Petrophile, is a shrub native to Western Australia. It is a member of the Proteaceae family and is often used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties. The leaves and flowers of the hairy Petrophile have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, coughs, and respiratory issues, thanks to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. Herbal preparations of Petrophile ericifolia subsp. subpubescens include infusions, teas, and tinctures, which can be used to support the immune system and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
